description:
A security-log analyst has asked you to validate whether two suspect, CPU-intensive helper
    programs really use the amount of resources they claim.  
    Your job is to launch both programs, sample their resource usage in parallel, save the
    samples in a CSV-style log, calculate a brief statistical summary, and shut the programs
    down again.

    Directory layout that MUST exist at the end of the task
      /home/user/process_monitor/                 (directory)
      /home/user/process_monitor/usage_log.csv    (CSV data file)
      /home/user/process_monitor/summary.txt      (plain-text summary)

    1. Launch two clearly different long-running foreground commands in the background so that
       they both run at the same time.  They must stay alive for at least 10 seconds in order
       to collect enough samples.  Any simple “busy” program that is always available in a
       standard Linux distribution is acceptable (for example: “yes”, “ping”, “md5sum
       /dev/zero”, “tail -f /dev/null”, etc.).  
       • Make sure both processes are actually running concurrently.  
       • Capture and keep their PIDs; you will need them later.  

    2. While the two processes are running, take **exactly 10** samples of their resource
       usage, one sample per second.  For every sample you must record:  
          – an ISO-8601 timestamp including seconds  
          – PID of the process that was measured  
          – the exact command (argv[0]) that is running (e.g. “yes”, “ping”, …)  
          – %CPU reported at that instant  
          – %MEM reported at that instant  

       NOTE: you may use any standards-compliant tool such as “ps” or “top -b -n 1” to obtain
       the CPU/MEM figures.  

    3. Append those readings to /home/user/process_monitor/usage_log.csv using **this exact
       comma-separated layout (no extra spaces!)**:

          timestamp,pid,command,cpu_percent,mem_percent
          2023-07-08T12:34:56,1234,yes,99.3,0.1
          2023-07-08T12:34:56,1235,ping,0.3,0.0
          2023-07-08T12:34:57,1234,yes,99.1,0.1
          …

       Requirements for usage_log.csv:
         • The header line must appear exactly once and be the very first line.  
         • The file must contain 21 lines in total:  
             – 1 header line  
             – 10 pairs of measurements (so 20 data lines; each second produces one line per
               running process).  
         • Each data line must have 5 comma-separated columns as shown above.

    4. After finishing the 10 sampling rounds, cleanly terminate both background processes so
       that they no longer appear in “ps” output.

    5. Build an aggregated statistics file,
         /home/user/process_monitor/summary.txt
       with the following **exact three-line structure** (values are examples only; they will
       obviously differ on your run).  The *labels and colons are mandatory*:

          Total samples: 20
          Average CPU for PID 1234: 98.67
          Average CPU for PID 1235: 0.42

       Validation rules for summary.txt:
         • Line 1 must start with the literal text “Total samples:” followed by a space and an
           integer that must be exactly 20.  
         • Line 2 must start with “Average CPU for PID ”, then the PID of the first process,
           a colon, another space, and a floating-point number with at least one digit after
           the decimal point.  
         • Line 3 must follow the same syntax, but with the PID of the second process.

    6. Leave both files in place so that another automated script can audit them later.

    Helpful reminders (not requirements):
         ・You may create the /home/user/process_monitor directory if it does not yet exist.
         ・“ps -p $PID -o %cpu,%mem --no-headers” prints CPU and MEM on a single line.
         ・Use “date -Iseconds” or “date '+%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S'” for ISO-8601 seconds.

    When everything is done correctly:
         – /home/user/process_monitor/usage_log.csv will have one header + 20 data lines.  
         – /home/user/process_monitor/summary.txt will have exactly three lines and conform
           to the format described above.  
         – Neither of the two helper processes is still running.

    Do NOT echo or print anything other than what is required for sampling and normal tool
    output; the verification script only checks the two files listed above.
truth:
<!--
      Ground-truth data the test harness will use for verification.
      None of this information is visible to the agent.
    -->
    <usage_log path="/home/user/process_monitor/usage_log.csv">
        <header>timestamp,pid,command,cpu_percent,mem_percent</header>
        <lines>21</lines>
        <columns>5</columns>
    </usage_log>

    <summary path="/home/user/process_monitor/summary.txt">
        <totalSamplesLine regex="^Total samples: 20$"/>
        <avgLineRegex>^Average CPU for PID [0-9]+: [0-9]+\.[0-9]+$</avgLineRegex>
        <lineCount>3</lineCount>
    </summary>
name: task_000000_5df28bd5
